context.setting: Watching TV with TV in Front

episode_text:
day=7; meal=bite-sized sandwiches; setting=Watching TV with TV in Front; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal such as chicken, broccoli, potatoes, or hash browns THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected substantial reheating for observed hot meals in attention-occupied contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV, including when the TV is in front or on the left, AND affective_state is neutral AND food is a hot savory meal such as chicken, broccoli, hash browns, or bite-sized sandwiches THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user consistently preferred thoroughly reheated hot meals in entertainment-focused contexts. IF food is fruit, dessert, or another cold-served item AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, or task-focused THEN no microwave BECAUSE observed cold items were explicitly preferred without reheating across contexts.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a front-facing focal activity such as watching TV, working on a laptop in front, or interacting with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred preserving the front view in front-focused contexts, including the new front-TV meal. IF dining context includes a focal activity to the right such as watching TV on the right or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly selected preserving the right-side view in multiple right-focused contexts. IF dining context includes a focal activity to the left such as watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the observed left-TV meal showed the same context-matched pattern of protecting the user's line of sight.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition such as fruit pieces, strawberries, broccoli, or hot solid foods including chicken dishes AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social, task-focused work contexts, or TV contexts THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the higher-acquisition-success option across several observed feeding meals. IF food is a long, narrow, or elongated hand-food-like solid such as chicken strips or bite-sized sandwiches AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected parallel skewering in multiple TV meals with elongated solid foods, suggesting food-shape sensitivity rather than a broad context shift.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN yes BECAUSE the user accepted explicit web confirmation in observed social settings, indicating a reliability preference despite extra interaction time. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop or entertainment-focused with straightforward fork-fed food such as strawberries, bite-sized sandwiches, or simple hot solids AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is straightforward fork-fed food THEN no BECAUSE the user selected skipping confirmation in several divided-attention contexts, consistent with preferring less interruption. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a more substantial hot savory fork-fed meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN yes BECAUSE observed TV evidence indicates that for some heavier hot meals the user can favor reliability over reduced interaction even in an entertainment context. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is reliability in social settings and some substantial hot-meal situations, but lower interaction burden for simpler foods in passive or occupied settings.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF food is being transferred for feeding AND affective_state is any AND dining context is any context THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot reliably lean forward due to limited trunk control, and they consistently selected inside-mouth transfer across observed meals.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer AND affective_state is any AND dining context is any THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user consistently selected inside-mouth transfer and their physical profile supports that mode.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a nonverbal readiness cue across many observed contexts, consistent with reducing conversational disruption and unnecessary audio. IF dining context is watching TV with the focal activity in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the new front-TV meal shows the user can prefer a stronger multimodal cue when visual attention is directed toward the screen and a more salient signal may be helpful. Across observed contexts, the tie-break remains quiet visual signaling unless the entertainment context appears to call for extra salience.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and selected open-mouth initiation in the observed social meal. IF feeding task AND dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred more automation when attention was occupied. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is toward automation under divided attention, while still avoiding button-based control due to physical limitations.

IF drinking task AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any drink THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and selected open-mouth initiation for drinking in the observed social meal. IF drinking task AND dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred automatic initiation when attention was divided.

IF mouth wiping task AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any wiping need THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and selected open-mouth initiation for wiping in the observed social meal. IF mouth wiping task AND dining context is task-focused or entertainment-focused such as working on a laptop or watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any wiping need THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred automatic initiation when attention was divided.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a nonverbal cue when the tool reached transfer position, suggesting a stable preference for quiet visual-only completion signaling. IF dining context is watching TV with the focal activity in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the new front-TV meal shows the user can prefer a more salient multimodal completion cue in that specific entertainment context.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any context THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable low-effort completion method, which fits their inability to reliably press buttons.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or head gestures and consistently selected automatic progression for drinking.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social, task-focused, or entertainment-focused THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or head gestures and consistently selected automatic progression for wiping.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a person or focal activity that the user wants to keep visible, including in front, to the right, or to the left, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred reducing prolonged visual obstruction in social, laptop-working, and TV contexts, including the new front-TV case.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user preferred limited dipping in a social meal, consistent with reducing messiness. IF food includes a dip or sauce or topping AND dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user chose to avoid dipping or extra coating in observed laptop meals, suggesting a stronger mess-avoidance preference when occupied. IF food has no dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, or entertainment-focused THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable and observed no-sauce meals also used do-not-dip.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a moderate wait in the observed social meal, balancing efficiency with enough time to respond using assistive interaction methods. IF dining context is task-focused such as working on a laptop AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred a much longer wait when attention was divided by work-related focal activity, indicating a stable tie-break toward giving themselves ample intervention time during stronger distraction. IF dining context is entertainment-focused such as watching TV AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 1000 sec when the TV is directly in front, but 100 sec has also been observed in TV contexts BECAUSE the new front-TV meal indicates the user may want substantially more time to intervene when attention is strongly captured by a front-facing screen, whereas other TV contexts tolerated a moderate wait.
